exercises.name: Lying Slider Runner
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure that you bridge up and keep your glutes active throughout the whole exercise.

2.Try not to let them sit down towards the mat as you alternate from left to right foot. 

3.You’ll also wanna keep your abdominals engaged to help preventing from lifting from your lower back.

4.Lie down on the floor and your feet on the slider. Your legs should be bent at the knees then push your hips up. This will be your starting position.

5. Slowly extend right knee until it is straight while keeping your hips up. Then back to starting position and extend the left knee.

6.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Lying Straight Leg Raise
exercises.description:
1. For this exercise, keep your core engaged, especially when you're lifting your feet up off the floor but also while they're returning and getting close to the ground again.

2. Lie with your back flat on a ground and your legs extended in front of you.

3. Place your hands on your sides with your palms down.

4. As you keep your legs extended, straight as possible, raise your legs until they make a 90-degree angle with the floor. Exhale as you perform this portion of the movement and hold the contraction at the top for a second.

5. As you inhale, slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.

exercises.name: Lying T-Bar Row
exercises.description: Rest your upper chest against the top of the pad and grab the handles. Pull the weight up and squeeze your back at the top of the movement. Pause, and return to the starting position.

exercises.name: m
exercises.description: Squeeze a ball or towel between knees. Breathe in and out and activate PFM. Place hands slightly under glutes , use your core to lift your butt up with a little bounce, place feet down and thrust hips up into a glute bridge. Keep your adductors tight and squeeze the ball or towel the entire time. Perform 5-10 and reset.

exercises.name: Machine Assisted Chin Up
exercises.description:
1. Start by kneeling on the pad of the assisted chin-up machine. Grab the handles with your palms facing towards you. 

2. Slowly lower yourself until your elbows are completely straight, and you're in a hanging position. 

3. Pull yourself up as high as you can under control. Repeat. 

4. Remember; the more weight you use with this exercise, the easier it is.

exercises.name: Machine Assisted Close Grip Pull Up
exercises.alternateName: Machine Assisted Neutral Grip Pull Up
exercises.description:
1) Select your appropriate weight for the exercise on the machine. Using more weight makes it easier. 

2) Grab the pull up bar with hands facing each other, on the narrow handles. Place your knees on the machine's rest pad and slowly load your body weight onto the machine. Let your arms go to full extension, and your body in is a straight line from hands to knees. 

3) Pull your body upwards until your nose is just over the level of the bar. 

4) Lower your body, returning to starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Assisted Dip
exercises.description:
1) Select desired weight and grip the dip bars with hands shoulder width apart and knees on support pad. 

2) Begin by lowering your body until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.

3) Pause and return to starting position by pushing up with your triceps. Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Assisted Parallel Grip Pull Up
exercises.alternateName: Narrow Grip Machine Assisted Pull Up
exercises.description:
1) Select your appropriate weight for the exercise on the machine. Using more weight makes it easier. 

2) Grab the pull up bar with fists facing one another. Place your knees on the machine's rest pad and slowly load your body weight onto the machine. Let your arms go to full extension, and your body in is a straight line from hands to knees.

3) Pull your body upwards until your nose is just over the level of the bar. 

4) Lower your body, returning to starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Assisted Wide Grip Pull Up
exercises.alternateName: Open Grip Machine Assisted Pull Up
exercises.description:
1) Select your appropriate weight for the exercise on the machine. Using more weight makes it easier. 

2) Grab the pull up bar with hands facing forward. Place your knees on the machine's rest pad and slowly load your body weight onto the machine. Let your arms go to full extension, and your body in is a straight line from hands to knees. 

3) Pull your body upwards until your nose is just over the level of the bar. 

4) Lower your body, returning to starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Back Extension
exercises.description: Adjust seat so back pad fits between shoulder blades. Legs should be slightly bent. Place feet firmly on foot pad and move torso back slowly. Pause at extended position.

exercises.name: Machine Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1. Start seated on the bicep curl machine.

2. Grasp the handles and curl your palms upwards using your biceps.

3. Pause, and return to starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Glute Kickback
exercises.description:
1. Start by resting your chest against the body pad, grasping the handles, with a single foot on the weight plate. 

2. Push the plate away, fully extending your knee and your hip. Squeeze the glutes for a moment, then return to the start position. 

3. Repeat on the other leg.

exercises.name: Machine Incline Chest Press
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you wanna set up the machine so that the handles are in-line with the middle of your chest at the bottom of the press.

2.Also, make sure that the weights don’t tap down in between every repetition.

3.Start by pressing handles out until arms are extended.

4.Return to starting position.

exercises.name: Machine Lateral Raise
exercises.description:
1. Start seated on the lateral raise machine. Place your hands on the handles, and keep your elbows in contact with the pads. 

2. Drive your elbows upwards and outwards until your upper arm is parallel to the floor. 

3. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Lying Leg Curl
exercises.description:
1.When performing the leg curl, what you wanna do is ensure that the bottom pad is just above the ankles and the top pad is above your knee. 

2.You don’t want the pad resting on your knee cap.

3.Also, ensure that the weights don’t tap down between your repetitions.

4. Keeping the torso flat on the bench, ensure your legs are fully stretched. 

5.Position your toes straight. This will be your starting position.

6.Begin by curling your leg up as far as you can.

7.Lower back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Machine Lying Single Leg Curl
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, you’ll be working the hamstrings.

2.Ensure that the top pad is above your knee cap to prevent injury.

3.Also, make sure the weights don’t tap down in between every single repetition.

4.Begin by curling your leg up as far as you can. 

5.Lower back to the starting position.

6.After you’re done one leg, switch and do the other leg.

exercises.name: Machine Preacher Curl
exercises.description: Sit with your upper arms resting on the preacher pad. Grip the bar and curl it toward your shoulders. Squeeze your biceps, then lower under control.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Abduction
exercises.description:
1.Sit upright in the machine with the leg pads at the outside of your knees with your knees together and your feet on the foot pegs.

2. Push your knees apart, bringing the pads away from each other.

3. Bring your knees back together, returning to starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Calf Raise
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, put the ball of your foot on the step. 

2.Place your lower thighs under the lever pad, which will need to be adjusted according to the height of your thighs. Now place your hands on top of the lever pad in order to prevent it from slipping forward. 

3.In order to get full range of motion, point your toe as far as it will go. And then, let the heel drop down as low as it will go.

4.Lift the lever slightly by pushing your heels up and release the safety bar. This will be your starting position.

5.Slowly lower your heels by bending at the ankles until the calves are fully stretched. Inhale as you perform this movement.

6.Raise the heels by extending the ankles as high as possible as you contract the calves and breathe out. Hold the top contraction for a second.

7.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Chest Fly
exercises.description:
1.Sit at the machine with your back flat on the pad. Both hands holding the handles with elbows slightly bent.

2.Push the handles together and squeeze your chest once your forearms reach the middle. Pause.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Chest Press
exercises.description:
1) Sit upright making sure back is rested on the backrest, both hands holding the horizontal position handles and close to your shoulders, elbows bent.

2) Push both handles forward to a straight arm position in front of your chest.

3) Bending at the elbows, slowly return to starting position.

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Dip
exercises.description:
1) Sit at the machine and grasp the hand grips which should be slightly above elbow level. 

2) Begin by pressing handles downwards until your arms are almost straight, pause, and return to starting position.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Dips
exercises.description: Sit on the dip machine with your hands on the handles and elbows bent. Push the handles down to straighten your arms, then slowly return to the start.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Hip Adduction
exercises.description:
1. Sit upright in the machine with the leg pads at the inside of your knees with your knees apart and your feet on the foot pegs.

2. Drive your knees inwards, bringing the pads close together in the middle.

3. Bring your knees outwards, returning to starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Leg Curl
exercises.description:
1.Adjust the machine lever to fit your height and sit on the machine with your back against the back support pad. Legs fully straight in front of you, back of lower legs on top of the padded lever. Grasp the side handles on the machine as you point your toes straight.

2.Curl your legs downward as far as possible.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Leg Extension
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, ensure that the pad rests just above the ankle.

2) Secondly, you wanna make sure that the weights don’t tap down in between each repetition.

3) Keep a tall body throughout the exercise. 

4) Extend your knees fully, raising your legs straight out.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Neutral Grip Row
exercises.description:
1.Sit at a reverse position in a machine, chest against the backrest. Hands holding the vertical position handle, arms extended.

2.Pull the handles towards your chest, bending at the elbows. Pause.

3.Slowly return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Neutral Grip Row
exercises.description:
1.Sit at a reverse position in a machine, chest against the backrest. Hands holding the vertical position handle, arms extended.

2.Pull the handles towards your chest, bending at the elbows. Pause.

3.Slowly return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Parallel Grip Chest Press
exercises.alternateName: Machine Seated Bench Press Parallel Grip Neutral grip
exercises.description:
1.Sit upright making sure back is rested on the backrest, both hands holding the handles and close to your shoulders, elbows bent.

2.Push both handles forward to a straight arm position in front of your chest.

3.Bending at the elbows, slowly return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Parallel Grip Shoulder Press
exercises.alternateName: Machine Seated Parallel Grip Overhead Press Neutral Grip
exercises.description:
1.Sit upright at a shoulder press machine, holding the inside handles at parallel grip position, fists facing each other and your elbows bent.

2.Press the handles overhead, extending your arms fully.

3.Lower the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Reverse Fly
exercises.description:
1.Sit at a reverse position in a machine, chest against the back rest. Both hands holding the handles, elbows slightly bent.

2.Move your arms outward and backward in a reverse fly motion until your elbows go just past your back. Pause.

3.Slowly return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1.Sit upright at a shoulder press machine, holding the inside handles at shoulder height with your palms facing in and your elbows bent.

2.Press the handles overhead, extending your arms fully.

3.Lower the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Single Arm Bicep Row
exercises.alternateName: Machine Seated Single Arm Underhand Row
exercises.description:
1.Sit at a reverse position in a machine, chest against the backrest. One hand holding the horizontal position handle in an underhand position, palm facing up.

2.Pull the handle into you and try to squeeze your shoulder blade in the back Pause.

3.Slowly return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Single Arm Chest Fly
exercises.description:
1.Sit at the machine with your back flat on the pad. One hand holding the handles with elbow slightly bent.

2.Push the handle, bringing it in front of your chest, squeezing it once your forearm reached the middle. Pause.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Single Arm Neutral Grip Row
exercises.description:
1.Sit at a reverse position in a machine, chest against the backrest. One hand holding the vertical position handle, arm extended.

2.Pull the handle towards your chest, bending at the elbow. Pause.

3.Slowly return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Single Arm Parallel Grip Chest Press
exercises.alternateName: single arm neutral grip chest press
exercises.description:
1.Sit upright making sure back is rested on the backrest, one hand holding the handle and close to your shoulders, elbow bent.

2.Push the handle forward to a straight arm position in front of your chest.

3.Bending at the elbow, slowly return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Single Arm Parallel Grip Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1.Sit upright at a shoulder press machine, holding the inside handle at parallel grip position, elbow bent.

2.Press the handle overhead, extending your arm fully.

3.Lower the handle returning to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Single Arm Reverse Fly
exercises.alternateName: Machine Seated Single Arm Rear Delt Fly
exercises.description: Sit facing the machine with one hand on the handle. With a slight bend in your elbow, sweep the arm out and back. Squeeze your shoulder blade at the top.
